title: Rich Velvety Ethiopian Lentils Misir Wot
description: A rich and velvety Ethiopian lentil dish made with tinned tomatoes, perfect for a brunch mood, and is diabetic friendly.
instructions: In a large earthenware bowl, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Add the berbere spice and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the crushed tinned tomatoes, salt, and black pepper, and stir well.
Add the rinsed and drained red lentils and water, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
Use an immersion blender to puree the lentils to a rich and velvety texture, or let it cool and puree it in a blender.
Serve the misir wot hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
